steps.title: Read the logical relationship between sentences
steps.content:
Sentence 1 makes a general claim: organisms have evolved surprising adaptations. Sentence 2 gives a specific case: tadpole shrimp embryos can pause development for over ten years during drought. The second sentence illustrates the first — it's an example, not a contrast, a simultaneous event, or a result.
  • Choice A — 'in contrast,': Signals opposition. Tadpole shrimp are not contrasted with the general claim — they support it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'for example,': Signals that a specific case illustrates a general point. Tadpole shrimp are exactly that — one specific example of a surprising adaptation. ✓
  • Choice C — 'meanwhile,': Signals that two things happen at the same time separately. The shrimp example is not a parallel event — it's evidence for the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'consequently,': Signals that something follows as a result. The shrimp's adaptation is not a consequence of the general claim — it illustrates it. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: "For example" is the only transition that signals illustration of a general principle with a specific case. That matches choice B — B is correct.
